title: quercus × columnaris
intro: Quercus × columnaris, also known as the Columnar Oak, is a hybrid oak tree resulting from the cross between the Quercus robur and Quercus petraea species. The bark of the Quercus × columnaris plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, improving digestion, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. The bark and leaves of the Quercus × columnaris can be prepared as infusions or decoctions for herbal use, or even dried and powdered for later consumption.
